methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
First Light Fusion, a Series C-stage nuclear energy company founded by Nicholas Hawker and Yiannis Ventikos, operates within a 10-company comparable universe of nuclear technology firms (3 public: OKLO, NNE, SMR; 7 private including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Last Energy). The company has raised $100M+ to date.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable methodology. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the company's $100M total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-subsequent-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $170M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while four same-sector comparable companies exist, insufficient data points meeting stage, timing, and valuation-tier filters (fewer than 3 datapoints) preclude reliable median benchmarking.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $144M (low) to $195M (high), with a median point of $170M. This range reflects the step-up multiplier applied to recent capital deployment, adjusted for stage-typical variance. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable-stage peers with published valuations, limiting cross-validation; the reliance on total capital raised as a proxy for prior round valuation; and sector-specific dynamics in nuclear technology commercialization that may diverge from broader venture patterns.
